content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Agreed with the planner on all four amendments after verifying the code. The three correctness/atomicity defects (fr-7-89ded87f, fr-7-8d13f7cc, fr-7-11d88bf9) are real bugs with concrete failure scenarios. The unused parameter issue (fr-7-5ecd5768) is a code hygiene observation, not a behavioral defect.

## Votes

- `fr-7-5ecd5768`: Reject
  Verified in fs.rs:3111-3157: the `_error_context` parameter is indeed unused, but the leading underscore is Rust's standard idiom for intentionally-unused parameters. The FS override uses a completely different recovery mechanism (pending sidecar discard at line 3146) instead of the trait default's CorruptRecord error path. This is a deliberate architectural choice, not a bug. No behavioral defect exists â€” the sidecar mechanism provides equivalent or better error handling. A clarifying comment would be nice but is not a correctness issue.
- `fr-7-89ded87f`: Accept
  Verified: `materialize_bundle` (service.rs:968-977) only rejects `Running` status, allowing `Completed` and `Failed` milestones through. `persist_plan_locked` (service.rs:1226-1254) then overwrites `plan_hash`, `plan_version`, and `progress` without any lifecycle status check. A completed/failed milestone can end up with a fresh unfinished plan, but `record_bead_start` (service.rs:1393-1400) still rejects terminal statuses via `is_terminal()`. This creates a semantically inconsistent state: the milestone references a new plan it can never execute. This is a real state machine defect.
- `fr-7-8d13f7cc`: Accept
  Verified: `record_bead_start` writes task-run lineage at service.rs:1404 before the snapshot+journal commit at service.rs:1444. If the snapshot write fails, the FS adapter discards the pending sidecar (fs.rs:3146) leaving the old snapshot intact, but the lineage row persists. On later completion, `build_reconciled_transition_events` (service.rs:684-722) only synthesizes `Planning -> Ready -> Running` bridges â€” it has no logic to bridge a missing `Ready -> Running` gap when status is `Ready` and target is `Completed` or `Paused`. This is a real durability/consistency defect that can cause finalization failures.
- `fr-7-11d88bf9`: Accept
  Verified: the default `commit_journal_ops` (service.rs:110-131) iterates ops one-by-one in a for loop. The default `commit_snapshot_and_journal_ops` (service.rs:138-165) only restores the previous snapshot on journal failure â€” it never attempts to undo already-applied journal ops. Any adapter or test double inheriting these defaults will leak partial journal writes when a later op fails, violating the atomic-commit contract. This is a real atomicity defect, not theoretical â€” any multi-op transition where a later op fails will produce inconsistent state.
